D9 BiologyL4 AnalyzeReference

Cuttlefish Chromatophore — Neural Expansion

cuttlefish skin holds millions of muscular sacs (chromatophores) — each one expanded by radial muscle pulls pigment from a small dot into a wide disc, painting the skin in milliseconds

Hanlon & Messenger 1996 traced the chromatophore neural circuit: brain motor neurons → radial muscles → pigment cell expansion. Each chromatophore is bidirectional: contracted = tiny dot (skin shows underlying iridophore); fully expanded = ~10× the area, pigment dominates. The fastest pattern change in any animal — under 100 ms.

Drag muscle activation; chromatophore array expands or contracts.
